resources.string: Randomly browse your gallery with one-handed gestures. Open Settings below to customize behavior.
Share this item to WeChat, QQ, X, and other apps through Android system share.
No app is currently available for sharing.
Customize gestures, delete behavior, display style, and language.
Delete requires "Enable delete action" in Settings. If it is off, delete gestures become next-photo swipes.
Want direct delete without Android confirmation? Turn on Silent delete in Settings.
Single tap photo: toggle card full-image mode (you can disable this in Settings).
Delete reminder can appear slightly later after a left swipe, because Android deletion needs a short moment to finish.

Two-finger controls: pinch to zoom the photo and drag with two fingers to move it inside the card. The card frame stays fixed, and a single tap resets zoom back to the original size. Tip: this panel auto-hides after appearing 5 times in one month, and auto-enables again next month. If you manually turn it off in Settings, it stays off next month too.
Only the photos Android currently shares are in this deck. Expand access if you want a wider shuffle.
Enabled. Any direction mapped to Delete will ask Android to remove that photo.
Disabled for safer browsing. Delete actions will fall back to next photo.
Enabled. Show a short reminder after a photo is deleted.
Disabled. No reminder is shown after deletion.
Enabled. Cards keep the entire photo visible with blank margins if needed.
Disabled (default). Cards use crop-fill for a fuller edge-to-edge look.
Enabled. Show the top-right menu button on the current card for sharing and quick actions.
Disabled. Hide the top-right menu button on the current card.
Enabled. Tapping the top photo switches between crop-fill and full-image display.
Disabled. Tapping the top photo does not switch display mode.
Enabled. A small red delete button appears at the lower-right and can be dragged after long-press.
Disabled by default. No floating delete button is shown.
Enabled. Slide from center to colored ring, preview action, then release to execute. Press and hold center to drag and reposition.
Disabled by default. Use regular card swipes only.
Enabled. Long-press drag and action target changes will play feedback.
Disabled. No gesture ball operation feedback is played.
Enabled. Shows action hint text while dragging.
Disabled. No floating action hint text is shown.
Enabled for %1$s. Photos inside this authorized folder are deleted silently after your swipe. Photos outside it still use Android confirmation.
Enabled, but required folders are not fully authorized yet. Please authorize both DCIM and Pictures for reliable screenshot and camera silent delete.
Authorized folder: %1$s. Turn on Silent delete if you want swipe-to-delete to skip the system confirmation inside this folder.
Disabled. Swipe-to-delete still uses Android confirmation. Silent deletion only works after you enable this option and authorize a folder.
Choose the action for each swipe direction.
Higher sensitivity starts and dismisses swipes with shorter finger movement. Lower sensitivity is more stable and less likely to trigger by mistake.
Open a concise explanation for gesture, display, delete, language, and update options.
Swipe behavior: assign each direction to previous, next, delete, or skip; sensitivity controls how far you must drag before a swipe confirms.
Display: full-image mode keeps the whole photo visible, while crop-fill makes cards feel fuller edge-to-edge.
Top-right actions button: controls whether the vertical three-dot menu appears on the current card.
Delete options: enable delete gestures, choose whether to show delete reminders, and optionally keep a floating delete button.
Gesture floating ball: preview action first, then execute on release; you can tune its size, feedback sound, and hint text.
Silent delete: after authorizing DCIM and Pictures folders, eligible photos can be removed without Android confirmation dialogs.
Language: switch between Arabic, English, Spanish, French, Russian, Chinese, or follow the system default.
Update: check manually, update now, or postpone reminders until a newer release is available.

1) Shuffle: swipe cards by your configured directions; on the top card, use two fingers to pinch-zoom and move the photo inside the fixed card frame, and single tap to reset zoom; or use the gesture floating ball when enabled.
2) Deletion: first turn on "Enable delete action". Without it, all delete gestures become next-photo.
3) Silent delete: turn it on, then authorize both DCIM and Pictures. We open each parent folder directly for quick setup.
4) If card movement feels off, tap Reshuffle once to rebuild the deck and continue smoothly.
5) Default controls panel: if it appears 5 times in one month, it auto-hides for the rest of that month and auto-enables next month. If you switch it off manually, it stays off next month too.
On. The panel can appear on startup. It auto-hides after 5 appearances in one month.
Temporarily hidden this month after appearing 5 times. It will auto-enable next month.
Off by you. It will stay off next month until you turn it on again.
A newer version (%1$s) is available. Your current version is %2$s.
You are already on the latest version.
You chose to skip update %1$s. You will be prompted again only when a newer release is available.
Unable to check or install update. Please try again later.
Next, Android will open folder authorization already pointed at the DCIM parent folder (includes Camera and many screenshot paths).
Next, Android will open folder authorization already pointed at the Pictures parent folder (covers many screenshot folders).
Tap "Use this folder" to authorize the whole DCIM library.
Tap "Use this folder" to authorize the whole Pictures library.
This media item couldn\'t be loaded.
You can keep swiping and the deck will move on to the next item.
The app is decoding a preview to keep swiping smooth and memory-safe.
Shuffle your gallery on your terms

Photo Roulette needs photo access to build a random deck from your library. On Android 14, you can allow all photos for the broadest shuffle, or share only selected photos and albums if that feels better.
Whatever you choose is valid. The app only shuffles and shows the photos Android lets it see, and nothing leaves your device.
Best random mix with full access
Giving access to all photos unlocks the widest range of surprises across your whole library.
Prefer a smaller set? Android 14 can limit access to only the photos or albums you choose, and Photo Roulette will stay inside that boundary.
You can revisit the permission choice later in system settings if you want to broaden or narrow what the app can shuffle.
A photo collection card in the permission illustration
A shuffle card in the permission illustration
A highlighted photo access card in the permission illustration
There are no photos left to browse right now. Take a few new shots and come back for another round.
Photo Roulette needs access to your photo library so it can build the shuffle deck. Open system settings to allow photo access for this app.
